HEADING: Canvas LTI Configuration
ACCESS_DENIED_MESSAGE: Your current package does not include Canvas LTI integration.
ACCESS_DENIED_DESCRIPTION: Canvas LTI integration enables FastComments commenting within Canvas LMS courses via the LTI 1.3 standard. You may need to contact support to enable this feature.
VALIDATION_PLATFORM_URL_REQUIRED: Canvas Platform URL is required when LTI is enabled.
VALIDATION_CLIENT_ID_REQUIRED: Client ID is required when LTI is enabled.
SAVE_FAILED: Failed to save Canvas LTI configuration.
HELP_ENABLE_LTI: Enable LTI 1.3 integration to embed FastComments in Canvas LMS courses.
HELP_PLATFORM_URL: The base URL of your Canvas instance (e.g. https://yourschool.instructure.com).
HELP_CLIENT_ID: The Client ID from the Canvas Developer Key you created for FastComments.
HELP_DEPLOYMENT_ID: Optional. The deployment ID assigned by Canvas when you add the external tool.
HELP_COMMENT_STYLE: Choose how students interact: threaded comments, real-time chat, or both.
LTI_URLS_DESCRIPTION: Use these URLs when configuring FastComments as an external tool in Canvas:
LABEL_CONFIG_URL: Configuration URL (paste into Canvas Developer Key):
SETUP_STEP_1: In Canvas, go to Admin > Developer Keys > + Developer Key > LTI Key.
SETUP_STEP_2: Select "Enter URL" as the configuration method and paste the Configuration URL above.
SETUP_STEP_3: Save the Developer Key and set its state to ON. Copy the Client ID shown in the Details column.
SETUP_STEP_4: Paste the Client ID into the field above and save this page.
SETUP_STEP_5: In Canvas, go to a course's Settings > Navigation tab > enable FastComments.
ROLE_MAPPING_DESCRIPTION: Canvas LTI roles are automatically mapped to FastComments permissions:
DOCUMENTATION_TEXT: Need help setting up Canvas LTI? Check out our Canvas Setup Guide or contact support for assistance.
